summary: The query asks for the EXCEPTION among options about giant cell tumor. While several features such as typical patient age range and potential pulmonary metastases are accurate, two statements stand out as false: claiming the matrix contains proliferating mononuclear cells (misidentifies tissue components; matrix comprises ECM, not cells) and attributing proliferative activity to osteoclast giant cells (proliferation occurs only in type I and II mononuclear cells). However, since the matrix does not consist of any cells at all but rather an extracellular substance, describing it as containing proliferating mononuclear cells constitutes a fundamental error in pathological nomenclature. This makes option B most clearly erroneous compared to other inaccuracies related to cell lineage. Hence, despite some overlap in falsity, B represents the strongest deviation due to confusion between cellular versus non-cellular structures.
